summary: Linked the Zebes route stage object by changing `melee/gr/grzebesroute.c` from NonMatching to Matching and finishing `grZebesRoute_8020B548`. The fix used inline helper wrappers, `HSD_ASSERT`, `__FILE__`, direct float/GXColor literals, and symbol-map demotions to local compiler labels. decomp.dev reported one new match: `main/melee/gr/grzebesroute` / `grZebesRoute_8020B548` improved from 99.97% to 100.00%, with linked code/data increases.

key_files.role: Main source changes; completed `grZebesRoute_8020B548`, removed fake globals for constants/assert strings, added inline helpers and direct literals.
Updated symbol scopes/labels for the newly linked object, including local `@NNN` constants and `...rodata.0` / `...data.0` labels.

what_changed: `grZebesRoute_8020B548` lost its nonmatching marker and gained a C implementation shape that matches: inline wrappers for `light_gobj->hsd_obj`, `HSD_LObjGetNext`, and `lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK`.
The light traversal loop was rewritten as a `for` loop with the `HSD_LObjGetNext` call in the update expression.
Explicit globals for `"grzebesroute.c"`, `"gobj"`, `"0"`, float constants, and `GXColor` constants were removed from `grzebesroute.c`.
Calls now use `__FILE__` and `HSD_ASSERT(0x169, gobj)` / `HSD_ASSERT(0x174, 0)` instead of manually calling `__assert` with named string globals.

Named constant references such as `grZe_Route_804DB918`, `grZe_Route_804DB92C`, and `grZe_Route_804DB944` were replaced with direct literals like `-50.0f`, `600.0f`, `500.0f`, `45.0f`, `0.99f`, and `0.03f`.
`GXColor` values are now local aggregate initializers inside the spot/point light branches.

`symbols.txt` demoted many former `grZe_Route_*` data symbols to local compiler-style labels such as `@187`, `@270`, `@211` through `@278`, and added local labels at `grZe_Route_803B83A0` and `grZe_Route_803E5DB0`.
decomp.dev reported `Matched code: 67.72% (+0.02%, +780 bytes)`, `Linked code: 39.11% (+0.05%, +1796 bytes)`, and `Linked data: 33.24% (+0.03%, +336 bytes)`.
smart_moves: Used tiny inline wrappers to influence MWCC code generation without changing the apparent logic of light-object access and traversal.
Switched from explicit `__assert` calls to `HSD_ASSERT`, letting the project macro supply the correct assert expansion and local strings.
Replaced named globals for constants with direct literals/local initializers, matching the compiler's emitted local constant style instead of preserving artificial global symbols.
Updated the symbol map in the same PR as the source match, preventing stale global `grZe_Route_*` labels from conflicting with compiler-generated local data.
Made the object Matching only after the last reported mismatch in `grZebesRoute_8020B548` was resolved.
decomp_lessons: For late-stage near-matches, very small C-shape changes such as inline helper wrappers can be enough to close the final byte-level gap.
If constants are compiler-emitted as local `@NNN` data, avoid keeping descriptive fake globals in C; direct literals and local aggregate initializers may be the correct representation.
`HSD_ASSERT(line, expr)` can match better than spelling out `__assert(__FILE__, line, string)` by hand, especially when assert expression strings are local data.
Loop form matters for matching: moving iterator advancement into the `for` update expression changed the code shape for the light-object traversal.
When linking an object, audit `symbols.txt` for stale global data names and convert them to local labels when the source no longer defines those globals.
PR evidence is mostly diff-based; the PR body was empty and there were no inline review comments explaining the author's reasoning.
naming_conventions: Stage route functions in this file use the prefix `grZebesRoute_` plus address-style suffixes, e.g. `grZebesRoute_8020B548`.
Existing global data names use the route abbreviation `grZe_Route_` plus address, e.g. `grZe_Route_804D6A60`.
Compiler-local data/constants are represented in `symbols.txt` as local labels like `@187`, `@270`, `@211`, `@278` rather than descriptive globals.
Local same-address section labels may appear as `...rodata.0` or `...data.0` beside a global object symbol.
The source uses `__FILE__` instead of a named file-string global for `grzebesroute.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added `inline HSD_LObj* grZebesRoute_8020B548_inline1(HSD_GObj*)` to cast `hsd_obj` to `HSD_LObj*`.
Used direct constants for light distances/attenuation and camera clamp values instead of loading from named `sdata2` globals.
Used branch-local `GXColor color = { ... }` initializers for spot, upper point, and lower point lights.
Changed assert sites to `HSD_ASSERT(0x169, gobj)` and `HSD_ASSERT(0x174, 0)`.
Converted the `while (i < 3 && lobj != NULL)` loop to a `for` loop with `i++` and next-light traversal in the update clause.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.note: Approved with no written review feedback.
PR body was empty, so rationale is inferred only from diff and bot report.

follow_up_queries: GXColor local initializer matching stage route lights
